element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic throughout; dual-character action-based battle system
Blended with menu-driven RPG mechanics as game progresses
Stat progression, items, and character advancement system
Higher encounter rate in US version than Japanese version; manageable early, escalates mid-game
Collectible progression items; fully obtainable by endgame
Location hinted by NPCs; required for progression
Accessible only with maxed stats and full equipment via developer password

typical_relief_triggers: Menu-driven RPG segments between action sequences
typical_tension_triggers: Random overworld encounters (higher frequency in US version)
Enemy party battles with action-based phase
Progressive difficulty scaling through stat requirements
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Hybrid action-RPG pacing: alternates between action-based combat intensity and menu-driven exploration/advancement; US version maintains higher encounter tension than Japanese version

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden NPCs and items (e.g., Dragstar location hints from NPCs in Chatzy town; Japanese version included Journey to the West-themed hidden characters removed in US localization)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory phase with safe action-based combat and basic RPG mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive difficulty through stat scaling; higher random encounter rates escalate tension
difficulty_curve.late_game: Requires maxed stats and full equipment to challenge final boss; developer 'FULL' password reveals endgame is fully accessible only at maximum progression
notable_spikes: US version: increased encounter rate compared to Japanese version creates sustained mid-to-late game tension
Final boss: positioned as culmination of stat progression system

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns serve as safe zones with NPC interactions (e.g., Chatzy) that provide hints and story progression; contrasts with overworld random encounter zones
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Chatzy (contains hidden NPCs in Japanese version; Dragstar hint location)

notable_level_patterns.description: Core design pattern blending real-time action sequences with traditional RPG menu navigation; unique to Culture Brain's RPG approach (continued from Kung-Fu Heroes predecessor)
Prism Bells and special items (Dragstar) serve as progression markers requiring exploration and NPC interaction; visible in inventory system and endgame completion state

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Little Ninja Brothers article)
